title: Lower Energy Costs Act
summary: A broad energy package that would speed up permitting for oil, gas, and mineral projects, expand domestic fossil-fuel and critical-mineral production, and roll back several fees and restrictions on energy development on federal land. Supporters frame it as lowering prices and boosting energy independence; critics argue it weakens environmental review and climate protections.

title: Parents Bill of Rights Act
summary: Requires public schools that receive federal funds to publish curricula, give parents the right to review instructional materials and budgets, be notified about violent incidents, and consent before changes to a minor's records. Supporters see it as parental transparency; opponents worry it invites book challenges and could out LGBTQ students.

title: Secure the Border Act of 2023
summary: Directs resumed construction of border barriers, raises standards for asylum claims, expands detention and expedited removal, and requires employers to use E-Verify. Supporters call it a restoration of border control; critics say it sharply curtails asylum access and humanitarian protections.

title: Freedom to Vote Act
summary: Sets nationwide election standards: automatic and same-day voter registration, at least two weeks of early voting, expanded mail voting, Election Day as a public holiday, and new transparency rules for political spending. Supporters frame it as protecting ballot access; opponents argue it federalizes elections that states should run.

title: Fiscal Responsibility Act of 2023
summary: Suspends the federal debt limit into 2025 while capping non-defense discretionary spending, clawing back some unspent pandemic funds, and adding work requirements to certain safety-net programs. A bipartisan compromise framed by supporters as avoiding default and restraining spending; critics on both sides say it either cut too much or too little.

title: Right to Contraception Act
summary: Establishes a statutory right to obtain and use contraceptives and for providers to supply them, and lets the Justice Department and individuals sue over state restrictions. Supporters frame it as safeguarding access nationwide; opponents raise religious-liberty and states'-rights objections.

title: Securing Growth and Robust Leadership in American Aviation Act
summary: Reauthorizes the Federal Aviation Administration for five years, funding air-traffic modernization, hiring and training more controllers, strengthening consumer refund and disability-access rules, and boosting safety oversight. A largely bipartisan infrastructure and safety bill.

title: Kids Online Safety Act
summary: Creates a duty of care for online platforms used by minors, requires stronger default privacy and parental-control settings, and lets young users opt out of algorithmic recommendation feeds. Supporters frame it as protecting children online; critics warn it could pressure platforms to over-remove lawful content and weaken privacy through age verification.
